scenes.title: Isolation and Pallor Acceleration

valueShift.end: I am dying alone and the anger isn't keeping me warm

scenes.title: The Bond Pulse

valueShift.end: She may have loved me AND been bound to kill me, and I don't know which is worse

craftToolsApplied.T5: Restrained tone — grief examined through architectural collapse metaphor
craftToolsApplied.T7: Black moment aftermath — anger cracking, simple betrayal narrative failing
craftToolsApplied.T10: Bond mechanics consistent — cannot fabricate emotion, reveals genuine grief

scenes.title: Hunter Encounter and Direction Change

valueShift.end: The mission requires her and the mission is all I have left

craftToolsApplied.T5: Action sequence maintains morally complex tone — no heroic framing
craftToolsApplied.T6: Stakes escalation — physical capability declining, timeline contracting
craftToolsApplied.T8: Self-sufficiency narrative collapses under practical reality
craftToolsApplied.T10: Pallor acceleration shown physically — cold in joints, delayed response
craftToolsApplied.T12: Fantasy action (vorakh fight) exposes consequences of romance separation
craftToolsApplied.T13: Tension level 8 maintained through physical danger and internal crisis

craftToolsApplied.T20: Emotional state: grim resolve with fragile hope underneath tactical framing

emotionalBeats: Direction change toward Lirien framed as mission necessity
Walking toward conversation she's not ready for

T5.notes: Tone morally complex throughout. No melodrama. Devastation conveyed through restraint and physical detail.

T6.notes: Stakes escalated from personal (isolation) through physical (Pallor acceleration, combat) to mission-critical (timeline contracted to days).

T7.notes: Black moment aftermath executed. Anger cracking. Simple betrayal narrative failing. Grief emerging beneath.

T8.notes: Internal resistance (Calwrath pattern, self-sufficiency) and external (Pallor, vorakh, contract timeline) both present and overcome through necessity.

T10.notes: Bond mechanics strictly consistent — cannot fabricate emotion, transmits genuine emotional quality, can be walled but not severed. Pallor acceleration follows established rules.

T12.notes: Fantasy threat (Pallor acceleration) directly caused by romance separation. Arcs fully coupled.

T13.notes: Tension level 8 maintained. Three distinct movements with rising stakes. Internal and physical pacing balanced.

T15.notes: FS-019 advanced (Kaelith thinks about secondary operative possibility). FS-020 advanced (deterioration timeline pressure). Direction change plants reunion setup for ch19.

T16.notes: Pallor motif present throughout — silver spreading on skin mirrors cosmic membrane failure. Fortification imagery dominates Kaelith's metaphor space. Water imagery deliberately absent except one instance of grief 'like floodwater' showing Lirien's influence.

T17.notes: Kaelith voice at maximum compression. 70%+ sentences under 10 words. Architectural metaphors exclusively. Emotion expressed through body. Longer sentences only when defences breach.

T18.notes: Information asymmetry maintained — Kaelith knows contract exists but cannot verify loophole validity. Bond transmits emotion, not facts.

T20.notes: Emotional arc: controlled anger → reluctant reassessment → grim resolve with fragile hope. Clear progression.

T21.notes: VL-12 collapse shown through Kaelith testing every ch15 memory for manipulation. Intimacy apex recontextualised as potential final stage of infiltration.

T23.notes: MB-011 (will trust survive) and MB-012 (real or binding) both active and advanced. Question not resolved but pushed toward eventual answer.

T24.notes: Minimal dialogue (none with other characters). Subtext in what Kaelith tells herself vs. what bond reveals — 'tactical monitoring' framing is transparent self-deception.

T25.notes: All three thematic pillars present. TP-1 (trust) examined from wounded side. TP-2 (right choice) tested — self-protection killing her faster. TP-3 (chosen vs bound) advanced — bond pulse forces question of genuine feelings within contract.

CHAR-001.chapterStart: Controlled anger — Calwrath pattern, military discipline applied to emotional survival, every memory under revision
CHAR-001.chapterEnd: Grim resolve with fragile hope — walking toward Lirien, framing as mission necessity, grief replacing anger

CHAR-002.statePerceived: Raw grief transmitting through bond — uncontrolled, patient, permanent weight. Not diminishing. Quality of loss not mission failure.

advanced.description: Court enforcement escalation — secondary operative possibility present in Kaelith's tactical assessment
advanced.lineRef: Internal reference to Court monitoring and enforcement escalation

advanced.description: 30/90 day deterioration timeline — Kaelith knows Lirien faces Lessening if contract unfulfilled
advanced.lineRef: Internal acknowledgment of Lirien's timeline pressure

continuityNotes.pallorProgression: Silver spread from wrist (ch16) to knuckles and elbow (ch17). Cold deepening. Timeline contracted from weeks to days. Two fingers non-functional by chapter end.
continuityNotes.bondState: Walled by Kaelith but not severed. Transmits Lirien's grief. Kaelith stops actively reinforcing wall in SCN-48 but does not reach back.
continuityNotes.locationProgression: Started heading east into Narrowing treeline, ended heading north toward Quietus/Lirien convergence
continuityNotes.physicalState: Bruised ribs (3rd/4th left, from ch14). Pallor-related cold and silver spreading. Combat-capable but degrading.

voiceMarkers.sentenceLength: 70%+ under 10 words. Longer sentences (15-25 words) appear only when emotional defences breach
voiceMarkers.metaphorDomain: Architecture, fortification, material science exclusively. Load-bearing walls, structures failing, cracks, weight
voiceMarkers.emotionalRegister: Suppressed-to-physical throughout. Anger = jaw tight, heat in neck. Grief = pressure behind sternum, weight across shoulders
voiceMarkers.thoughtPatterns: Linear tactical assessment applied to emotional situations. Under stress: fragments to inventory mode
voiceMarkers.waterImagery: One deliberate exception — grief arriving 'like floodwater through a crack' — showing Lirien's influence leaking through
voiceMarkers.distinctFromLirien: No water-as-identity metaphors, no precise spacing between words, no operative taxonomies, no fae distance

hookAndClose.openingHook: Imperative voice hook — 'Fire first. Perimeter second. Don't think about the rest.' Establishes military discipline masking devastation.
hookAndClose.closingTechnique: Forward motion with unresolved tension — walking toward conversation she's not ready for, but walking anyway. Hope fragile but present.
